TextENGNew: ``Goodbye, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS09/02/170201649_2. ...You can still apologize to me, you know?``
changeReason: The original adds 'when you have the chance' (implying a future opportunity) not in the Japanese; the source 謝ってもいいよ？ is a gentle, almost teasing offer ('you can apologize, you know?') with a questioning tone that the new version captures better.
The original uses 'Fuck' which is stronger profanity than the Japanese ちきしょう (damn it/dammit), escalating the vulgarity beyond what the source text conveys.

TextENGNew: This was no time to be wasting around here.
changeReason: The original uses past tense 'I wasn't supposed to be' framing it as a reflection on past error, while the Japanese 場合じゃない expresses present-tense urgency ('this is not the situation for'), which the new version captures.

TextENGNew: If I don't hurry, ...the timed incendiary device Rena planted is going to......!!!
changeReason: The original translates 時限発火装置 (timed incendiary/ignition device) as 'explosive device,' which describes a different type of weapon; the Japanese specifically refers to a fire-starting device, not a bomb.

TextENGNew: And then, just as Satoko let out that anguished scream, it happened.
changeReason: The original translates 悲痛な (anguished/grief-stricken) as 'desperate,' which conveys urgency/hopelessness rather than the grief and pain the Japanese expresses. The new version also completes the sentence with 'it happened' rather than leaving it as a fragment.

TextENGNew: Everything before my eyes vanished from the world.
changeReason: The original adds 'into the void' as a destination not present in the Japanese source, which simply states everything disappeared from the world before his eyes. This addition introduces a metaphysical element absent from the original.
